info.name: Super Banking — Advanced Utilities
info.description:
Advanced utility requests for Super Banking delegated token flows.

Requires: BX-Finance-Shared.postman_environment.json imported and active in Postman.

PAZ Policy Decision: Tests the PingOne Authorize (PAZ) policy engine against an MCP token. Set PAZ_DECISION_ENDPOINT_ID in the shared env (PingOne Admin → Authorize → Decision Endpoints → copy the UUID). Requires an MCP token in {{mcp_exchanged_token}} — run the 2-Exchange pi.flow collection first (Step 6b saves the token).

Token Revocation: Revokes an MCP token using the MCP service client credentials. Set the token to revoke in {{token_to_revoke}} collection variable before running.

variable.description: PingOne Authorize (PAZ) decision endpoint UUID — from PingOne Admin → Authorize → Decision Endpoints. Also set in BX-Finance-Shared.postman_environment.json.
Runtime: the MCP exchanged token. Populated by Step 6b test script in the 2-Exchange pi.flow collection.
Runtime: paste the token you want to revoke here before running the Token Revocation request.

header.description: MCP exchanged token — run 2-Exchange pi.flow first to populate this variable

request.description:
Calls the PingOne Authorize (PAZ) decision endpoint to test policy evaluation.

The MCP token is passed both as the Bearer token (for PAZ authentication) and as inMCPTokenJWT (for policy evaluation against the token claims).

Expected response: 200 with a 'decision' field (PERMIT or DENY).

Prerequisites:
1. Run the 2-Exchange pi.flow collection first — Step 6b saves the MCP exchanged token to {{mcp_exchanged_token}}.
2. Set PAZ_DECISION_ENDPOINT_ID in the shared env (PingOne Admin → Authorize → Decision Endpoints → copy the UUID from the endpoint URL).

exec: throw new Error('[PAZ Policy Decision] mcp_exchanged_token is not set — run the 2-exchange pi.flow collection first (Step 6b saves the token).');
throw new Error('[PAZ Policy Decision] PAZ_DECISION_ENDPOINT_ID is not set — find it in PingOne Admin → Authorize → Decision Endpoints and set it in the shared environment.');

urlencoded.description: The token to revoke — paste it into the token_to_revoke collection variable
MCP Service OAuth2 client ID — from shared environment
MCP Service OAuth2 client secret — from shared environment

request.description:
Revokes an MCP token using the MCP service client credentials.

Set the token you want to revoke in the {{token_to_revoke}} collection variable before running.

Expected response: 200 with empty body (token revoked successfully).

The token is no longer valid after this call — subsequent introspection will show 'active: false'.

Prerequisites: MCP_CLIENT_ID and MCP_CLIENT_SECRET must be set in the shared environment.

exec: throw new Error('[Token Revocation] token_to_revoke is not set — paste the token you want to revoke into the token_to_revoke collection variable.');

request.description:
Returns recent MCP tool-call audit events. The BFF proxies this from the MCP server audit endpoint.

Optional query params:
- agentId: filter by agent
- operation: filter by tool name
- eventType: tool_call | auth_challenge | consent_request | session
- outcome: success | failure | challenge
- limit: max 200 (default 50)

Returns empty events array gracefully when MCP server is unreachable. Added Phase 34.

Returns the current token exchange mode.

Response: { mode: '1-exchange' | '2-exchange' }

- 1-exchange: Single RFC 8693 token exchange (user token → MCP token)
- 2-exchange: Chained delegation (user token → agent token → MCP token)

Sets the token exchange mode.

Body: { mode: '1-exchange' | '2-exchange' }

No session or authentication required. Change the body value to switch modes:
- '1-exchange': Use single RFC 8693 token exchange
- '2-exchange': Use 2-hop chained delegation
